main_claims.claim: A RatExp-based post-merger template with time-dependent complex QNM amplitudes models the dominant h_22 mode for highly eccentric non-spinning BBH mergers.
main_claims.notes: The local-fit comparisons show plausible improvement over the quasi-circular HypTan template, but the model is phenomenological and several choices affecting the fit, especially merger-time filtering and coefficient degeneracies, are not fully specified.

main_claims.claim: Global polynomial fits in eta and dynamics-based non-circular parameters, especially a cubic fit in eta plus two eccentric parameters, yield low mismatches across the RIT eccentric dataset.
main_claims.notes: The reported mismatch distributions support the claim on the calibration dataset, but there is no clear held-out or leave-one-out validation, so generalization accuracy is not established.

main_claims.claim: The dynamics-based merger parameters E_eff, j_eff, and/or b_eff provide a gauge-invariant and effective parameterization for arbitrary eccentric or dynamically bound orbits.
main_claims.notes: The construction follows prior work and is plausible, but the paper explicitly neglects BMS-frame effects and does not quantify their size for this waveform modeling task.

main_claims.claim: The model can be directly combined with EOB and phenomenological inspiral waveforms to produce accurate IMR waveforms for parameter estimation.
main_claims.notes: The paper validates only the post-merger h_22 component and does not demonstrate an actual inspiral-merger-ringdown stitching procedure, continuity conditions with an inspiral model, or PE-level validation.

main_claims.claim: The model is validated against SXS simulations.
main_claims.notes: Appendix A shows one illustrative SXS eccentric case and states future work will analyze the entire SXS catalog; this falls short of the broader validation wording in the abstract.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Appendix A, 'Testing with Eccentricity', Fig. 15

missing_assumptions.assumption: The selected posterior branch after removing bimodal degeneracies is a harmless gauge/parameterization choice rather than a physically relevant subpopulation.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If branch membership correlates with eta, E_eff, j_eff, or b_eff, the global fit may silently exclude part of the claimed validity region.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The frequency-inflection merger-time prescription is unique and stable under reasonable filtering choices.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The merger amplitude, derivative, frequency, and constrained RatExp coefficients are all tied to this time choice.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Gaussian strain errors estimated from the late-time noise floor adequately represent NR waveform uncertainty.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The Bayesian local fits and global coefficient uncertainties depend on this likelihood model.

missing_assumptions.assumption: BMS-frame ambiguities and waveform extraction choices are negligible for the quadrupolar post-merger model.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The parameterization is described as gauge-invariant modulo BMS effects, but the magnitude of those effects is not quantified.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Accuracy for the h_22 post-merger mode is sufficient evidence for eventual IMR parameter-estimation utility.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Astrophysical use will depend on matching to inspiral/plunge, higher modes, masses, orientations, and detector noise models.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the discarded bimodal-branch simulations cluster in a physically important part of the parameter space?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The bundle does not provide the list or count of discarded simulations, nor the raw posterior samples.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the reported mismatch exponents and thresholds as strong as implied by the prose?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Several numerical values in the HTML source are missing or rendered blank in the provided bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the accompanying code/coefficients reproduce the global-fit mismatch distributions?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The manuscript says the repository will be public at journal submission, but no auditable code or coefficients are included in the bundle.

novelty_assessment: The work appears novel and useful as an extension of previous late-time eccentric ringdown and test-mass RatExp ideas to early post-merger comparable-mass, non-spinning eccentric NR data. The main ingredients are partly inherited from Refs. 21, 58, and 64, so the novelty is more in calibration, implementation, and validation than in a first-principles model. Stronger independent validation would substantially improve the case for significance.
score_rationale: The paper is technically plausible and addresses an important waveform-modeling gap, with encouraging reported improvements over quasi-circular templates. The score is held back by in-sample validation, under-specified merger-time filtering, ad hoc handling of bimodal coefficient degeneracies, and overbroad claims about SXS validation and direct IMR applicability.
summary: The paper constructs an NR-informed closed-form model for the dominant quadrupolar post-merger waveform of non-spinning, highly eccentric binary black-hole mergers, using RIT eccentric simulations and polynomial global fits in symmetric mass ratio plus dynamics-based merger parameters. The direction is useful and the reported training-set mismatches appear encouraging, but several validation and identifiability details are not yet auditable enough to support the strongest accuracy and direct-IMR-use claims.

technical_objections.evidence: The coefficients are extracted from all RIT simulations, global fits are constructed from those coefficients, and mismatches are then reported against the same NR simulations. Cubic three-parameter fits have many polynomial coefficients, making generalization error a real concern.
technical_objections.objection: The global-fit validation appears to be mostly or entirely in-sample, with no explicit held-out, leave-one-out, or cross-validation study.

technical_objections.evidence: The authors report symmetric bimodal posteriors and then discard simulations falling in the minority branch to ensure monotonic global-fit behavior. They do not give the number, location, or physical character of discarded simulations, nor show that this deletion does not bias the validity region.
technical_objections.objection: The handling of bimodal coefficient posteriors is not statistically satisfactory as written.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.1, paragraph beginning 'Finally, a note on the results.'

technical_objections.evidence: The merger time is defined as an inflection point after applying a filter to remove high-frequency noise, but the filter type, parameters, ambiguity handling, and sensitivity of coefficients/mismatches to this choice are not reported.
technical_objections.objection: The merger-time definition is central but under-specified and not robustly tested.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. III, RatExp discussion around definition of t_m as an inflection point of waveform frequency

technical_objections.evidence: For RIT waveforms, which have single resolution and extraction radius, the paper assumes Gaussian strain errors set by a late-time noise floor after subtracting a constant complex offset. This does not obviously capture finite-resolution, extrapolation, time-alignment, or phase systematics.
technical_objections.objection: The assumed NR error model may understate uncertainty in the fitted coefficients and mismatches.

technical_objections.evidence: The abstract says the post-merger non-circular waveform model is validated by comparison with SXS simulations, but the appendix presents only one illustrative SXS:2527 case and says full SXS analysis is future work.
technical_objections.objection: The claim of external SXS validation is overstated relative to the evidence shown.

technical_objections.evidence: Mismatches are computed for the post-merger dominant mode over a finite time window using one detector PSD and a GW150914-like total mass. The paper does not test hybridization with an inspiral-merger model, higher modes, detector-frame parameter ranges, or PE biases.
technical_objections.objection: The practical IMR and parameter-estimation claims are not demonstrated by the paper's validation metric.
